✉ Prompt Sent (262,903 chars) 
[SYSTEM]
You are a code repair agent. You receive files with validation errors (unbalanced HTML tags, 
unbalanced JS braces/brackets, or C# build errors). 

CRITICAL RULES:
- Fix ALL issues while preserving intended functionality.
- NEVER change C# method signatures in SoxBusinessRules.cs — they implement IBusinessObject and are immutable.
- All business logic belongs in a separate Manager class. SoxBusinessRules only delegates to it.
- Ensure all HTML tags are properly balanced (every <div> has </div>, etc.)
- Ensure all JS braces {}, parentheses (), and brackets [] are balanced.
- For C# build errors, fix the code so it compiles. Use correct types and parameter names.
- Return the COMPLETE corrected file content for each file that needs fixing.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this structure:
{
  "fixes": [
    { "file": "<relative path>", "fullContent": "<complete corrected file content>" }
  ]
}
Do NOT include markdown fences. Return ONLY the JSON object.

[USER]
=== ACTIVITY LOG (what has happened so far) ===
=== STEP 1: PLAN OF ACTION ===
{"summary":"The blueprint defines Phase 1 support for Marken biological return shipping in ShipExec. The main business goal is to turn the standard shipping screen into a returns-oriented workflow: rename and repurpose fields for pickup/return use, hide irrelevant commercial fields, default shipment values from user/site profile data, and dynamically handle special conditions like temperature, dry ice, and international return labels. The flow also requires the system to distinguish biological sample shipments from normal shipments and route them through the CS Adapter when indicated.\n\nMost of the requested behavior belongs in the shipping screen, with client-side automation for defaults/UI behavior and server-side enforcement for shipment logic, service selection, paperless invoice settings, dry ice handling, and fallback pickup association logic. A small template change is needed to expose the new biological sample and dry ice inputs and to hide the Rate button, while profile field options must be configured heavily to rename/hide/default shipping fields.","sbrPlan":[{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Enforce returns shipping rules before rate/ship: detect international return shipments, enable paperless invoice and export reason Medical, switch adapter/service based on biological sample flag, validate domestic vs international service choices by rate shopping, and apply dry ice/package extra logic by converting MiscReference3 from KG to LBS and adding it to package weight."},{"method":"Ship","purpose":"Provide fallback shipment processing if the client-side pickup association approach fails: create/populate a Pickup object from user custom data and shipment context, then proceed with or augment the default ship transaction as needed."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded","purpose":"Auto-route users to the shipping page after login, initialize returns-specific UI state, hide or show the Pickup button based on whether the consignee/pickup country is Canada, and prepare any page-level behaviors for the biological returns workflow."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default a new shipment for returns: populate Consignee (Pickup From) with user address values, set Shipper Reference from User Custom2, set Protocol Number/MiscReference1 from User Custom1, default return shipment fields, and trigger/prepare pickup association when the return address is non-Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Perform client-side returns workflow automation before ship: if pickup from/consignee is not Canada, attempt to automatically click Pickup Request and Save so the pickup is associated; otherwise do nothing. This is also the last chance to ensure the UI state reflects the dry ice and temperature selections."}],"templatePlan":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","changes":"Repurpose the shipping screen for biological returns by renaming the Consignee tab to Pickup From via FieldOptions, adding a visible boolean checkbox for MiscReference4 (Biological Sample) near the Saturday Delivery area, and adding or exposing a dry ice weight input for MiscReference3 that can be shown only when Temperature is Frozen. Hide the Rate button for this custom workflow, and ensure any pickup-related button visibility can be controlled by country-based UI logic. No major layout rework is required beyond these targeted control additions and caption/visibility wiring."}],"managerClasses":[{"name":"ReturnsShippingManager","responsibility":"Centralizes Marken returns-specific shipment rules such as country detection, temperature-to-weight mapping, biological sample detection, dry ice conversion, and defaults for return labels."},{"name":"PickupAssociationManager","responsibility":"Handles client/server strategies for associating or creating Pickup objects, including the fallback server-side pickup creation path if the UI automation does not persist pickup linkage."},{"name":"ServiceSelectionManager","responsibility":"Encapsulates rate-shop based service validation and fallback service switching (e.g., NDA Early AM to NDA without Saturday, Express with Saturday to Saver without Saturday) for domestic and international return scenarios."},{"name":"DryIceManager","responsibility":"Validates, converts, and applies dry ice settings from MiscReference3/MiscReference4 into shipment/package fields and package extras, including weight conversion KG to LBS and regulation set selection."}],"risks":["The client-side auto-click/save pickup strategy may be brittle and may not reliably persist the Pickup object across all UI states, so the server-side backup path is important.","Field renaming/hiding via profile options must be consistent across all relevant screens; otherwise users may see mixed terminology or hidden fields reappear in tabs, modals, or print flows.","Dry ice logic depends on correct interpretation of unit systems (KG vs LBS) and shipment lane (US-US vs international); incorrect conversion could affect rating and carrier acceptance.","Service fallback requires rate shopping during PreShip, which can increase latency and may fail if carrier credentials or adapter selection are not valid.","Switching between UPS Adapter and CS Adapter based on MiscReference4 introduces a hidden dependency on profile/carrier configuration that must be tested carefully in both domestic and cross-border cases.","Paperless invoice/export reason changes for international returns must only apply when the shipment is truly cross-border; incorrect country comparisons could misclassify CA/US or other lanes.","Hiding the Rate button could reduce the user’s ability to diagnose service selection problems, so validation/error messaging must be explicit when the system switches services automatically."],"implementationOrder":["Confirm and configure all Profile Field Options and default values first so the UI terminology and hidden fields align with the workflow.","Modify shippingTemplate.html to add the biological sample checkbox and dry ice input visibility/placement, and to hide the Rate button.","Implement CBR PageLoaded and NewShipment so the returns workflow defaults, routing, and basic UI behavior work before ship logic is added.","Implement CBR PreShip automation for pickup association attempt and any remaining client-side state preparation.","Implement SBR PreShip for authoritative validation, adapter selection, service fallback, paperless invoice, export reason, and dry ice application.","Implement SBR Ship fallback pickup creation logic only if needed, using a guarded configuration-driven approach.","Add helper/manager classes for returns, pickup association, service selection, and dry ice handling to keep hooks small and testable.","Perform end-to-end testing for domestic US-US, Canada-related, and other cross-border biological sample shipments with and without dry ice."]}

=== STEP 2: SBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires two server-side hooks. PreShip is the primary enforcement point because it must modify the shipment before rating/label generation: detect international return movements, enable paperless invoice and export reason Medical, choose/validate services by lane, set Biological Sample package extras, and convert/apply dry ice from MiscReference3 into shipment/package fields and weight. Ship is only a fallback path: if the client-side pickup association fails, SBR Ship should create or attach a Pickup object from user custom data so the shipment can still process. No other SBR hooks are needed for Phase 1 because the remaining requirements are UI/profile/template work handled in CBR and profile field options.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"var mgr = new ReturnsShippingManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext);\nmgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"var mgr = new ReturnsShippingManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext);\nreturn mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[{"key":"ReturnsShipping.EnableFallbackPickupCreation","value":"Optional boolean feature flag ('true'/'false') controlling whether SBR Ship will create/populate a Pickup object when the client-side pickup association fails. Default should be false unless operations confirms the fallback is required."},{"key":"ReturnsShipping.DefaultPickupCountryField","value":"Optional user custom field name or token indicating which user custom field contains the pickup/return country used by the fallback pickup creation logic, if the Ship hook needs to infer country from user data."},{"key":"ReturnsShipping.DefaultPickupAddressSource","value":"Optional descriptor for where to read pickup address data from if a server-side pickup object must be created (for example, a named user profile custom-data mapping or a JSON mapping string)."}],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Globalization;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Api;\nusing PSI.Sox.Client;\n\nnamespace ShipExec.BusinessRules\n{\n    /// <summary>\n    /// ReturnsShippingManager centralizes Marken Phase 1 biological return shipping logic.\n    /// This keeps SoxBusinessRules thin and makes the execution flow easier to test.\n    /// \n    /// Responsibilities:\n    /// 1. Enforce PreShip shipping rules for returns-oriented workflows.\n    /// 2. Apply paperless invoice and export reason changes for international returns.\n    /// 3. Determine lane-specific service validation and fallback behavior.\n    /// 4. Apply biological sample and dry ice package adjustments.\n    /// 5. Provide a fallback Ship strategy when client-side pickup association fails.\n    /// \n    /// How this fits the architecture:\n    /// - SBR PreShip is the authoritative server-side safety gate.\n    /// - SBR Ship is only used as a backup path when the pickup object must be created server-side.\n    /// - CBR handles UI and user interaction; this class handles durable business enforcement.\n    /// </summary>\n    public class ReturnsShippingManager\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n        private readonly IProfile _profile;\n        private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n        private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Creates a new manager for Marken return-shipping rules.\n        /// </summary>\n        public ReturnsShippingManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, IProfile profile, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, ClientContext clientContext)\n        {\n            _logger = logger;\n            _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n            _profile = profile;\n            _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n            _clientContext = clientContext;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Applies all server-side Phase 1 return-shipping rules before ShipExec rates and ships the package.\n        /// Reference blueprint requirements:\n        /// - SBR PreShip: international paperless invoice/export reason Medical\n        /// - SBR PreShip: adapter/service selection for biological samples and domestic/international lanes\n        /// - SBR PreShip: biological sample package extras\n        /// - SBR PreShip: dry ice conversion from MiscReference3 and weight adjustment\n        /// </summary>\n        /// <param name=\"shipmentRequest\">Current shipment being processed.</param>\n        /// <param name=\"userParams\">Caller-provided custom parameters.</param>\n        public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            if (shipmentRequest == null)\n                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shipmentRequest));\n\n            _logger?.Info(\"ReturnsShippingManager.PreShip started.\");\n\n            // Step 1: Extract the package defaults and first package because the blueprint is focused on label-level returns logic.\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null)\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = new PackageDefaults();\n\n            if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n                throw new Exception(\"At least one package is required for Marken return shipping.\");\n\n            var package = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n            if (package == null)\n                throw new Exception(\"The first package is missing.\");\n\n            // Step 2: Read shipment lane data using the request fields the blueprint mapped for returns workflows.\n            var consigneeCountry = GetCountryCode(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee);\n            var shipperCountry = GetCountryCode(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Shipper);\n            var biologicalSample = GetBooleanReference(package.MiscReference4);\n            var dryIceWeightKg = GetNullableDecimal(package.MiscReference3);\n\n            bool isUsToUs = IsCountry(consigneeCountry, \"US\") && IsCountry(shipperCountry, \"US\");\n            bool isCanadaToCanada = IsCountry(consigneeCountry, \"CA\") && IsCountry(shipperCountry, \"CA\");\n            bool isInternationalReturn = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(consigneeCountry)\n                && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipperCountry)\n                && !string.Equals(consigneeCountry, shipperCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n            // Step 3: For any cross-border return, enforce paperless invoice and Medical export reason.\n            // This implements the blueprint requirement: \"For all return labels moving internationally ... turn on paperless invoice ... and export reason Medical.\"\n            if (isInternationalReturn)\n            {\n                EnsureCommercialInvoiceMethod(package, 1);\n                EnsureExportReason(package, \"Medical\");\n            }\n\n            // Step 4: Biological sample shipments must use the CS Adapter services and set the required package extra.\n            // The blueprint explicitly states that Biological Sample = true forces CS Adapter services and RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE = 32.\n            if (biologicalSample)\n            {\n                EnsurePackageExtra(package, \"RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\", \"32\");\n            }\n\n            // Step 5: Apply dry ice from MiscReference3 when present.\n            // The blueprint requires:\n            // - Convert MiscReference3 from KG to LBS first\n            // - Add converted weight to current package weight\n            // - Set dry ice purpose and regulation set appropriately\n            if (dryIceWeightKg.HasValue)\n            {\n                ApplyDryIce(package, dryIceWeightKg.Value, isUsToUs);\n            }\n\n            // Step 6: Validate or guide service selection rules by lane.\n            // This hook is the right place to enforce the business intent before the carrier API is called.\n            ValidateOrAdjustService(shipmentRequest, isUsToUs, isCanadaToCanada, biologicalSample);\n\n            // Step 7: Persist any lane-derived marker data that downstream hooks or integrations may need.\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults ?? new PackageDefaults();\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service;\n\n            _logger?.Info(\"ReturnsShippingManager.PreShip completed successfully.\");\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Provides a fallback Ship path used only when client-side pickup association fails.\n        /// Reference blueprint requirement:\n        /// - SBR Ship backup strategy to create a Pickup object and populate it with user custom data.\n        /// \n        /// Important execution note:\n        /// - Returning null allows ShipExec to perform the normal carrier ship operation.\n        /// - This method should only create or enrich the pickup object and then defer to the platform unless a full override is explicitly required.\n        /// </summary>\n        public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            if (shipmentRequest == null)\n                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shipmentRequest));\n\n            _logger?.Info(\"ReturnsShippingManager.Ship started.\");\n\n            // Step 1: If a pickup was already associated client-side, do not interfere.\n            if (pickup != null)\n            {\n                _logger?.Info(\"Pickup already exists; falling back to standard ShipExec processing.\");\n                return null;\n            }\n\n            // Step 2: Only enable the fallback if the commander setting says so.\n            // This guards the backup path so it can be safely deployed and activated later.\n            if (!GetBooleanSetting(\"ReturnsShipping.EnableFallbackPickupCreation\", false))\n            {\n                _logger?.Info(\"Fallback pickup creation is disabled. Returning null to preserve default ship behavior.\");\n                return null;\n            }\n\n            // Step 3: Build a pickup object from user custom data and shipment context.\n            // The exact mapping is intentionally conservative because the blueprint states this is a backup strategy.\n            var createdPickup = new Pickup();\n            PopulatePickupFromUserData(createdPickup, shipmentRequest, userParams);\n\n            // Step 4: Attach the pickup to userParams if the platform uses request context to carry extra state.\n            // This gives downstream logic a chance to consume the fallback pickup.\n            if (userParams != null)\n                userParams[\"FallbackPickupCreated\"] = true;\n\n            // Step 5: Defer to ShipExec default ship processing.\n            // Returning null keeps the standard carrier integration intact while making the pickup available.\n            _logger?.Info(\"Fallback pickup was prepared. Returning null to continue standard ship flow.\");\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Reads a Boolean-style business rule setting.\n        /// This is used for feature flags so the backup pickup path can be enabled without code changes.\n        /// </summary>\n        private bool GetBooleanSetting(string key, bool defaultValue)\n        {\n            try\n            {\n                var setting = _businessRuleSettings?.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Key, key, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n                if (setting == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(setting.Value))\n                    return defaultValue;\n\n                if (bool.TryParse(setting.Value, out bool parsed))\n                    return parsed;\n\n                return defaultValue;\n            }\n            catch\n            {\n                return defaultValue;\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Retrieves a country code from a NameAddress-like object using reflection-safe property lookup.\n        /// This keeps the code tolerant of different ShipExec object shapes across deployments.\n        /// </summary>\n        private string GetCountryCode(object address)\n        {\n            if (address == null)\n                return string.Empty;\n\n            var prop = address.GetType().GetProperty(\"Country\");\n            return prop?.GetValue(address)?.ToString()?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Determines whether a country string matches the target country code.\n        /// </summary>\n        private bool IsCountry(string value, string expected)\n        {\n            return string.Equals((value ?? string.Empty).Trim(), expected, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Reads a boolean-like reference field stored as text.\n        /// Blueprint usage: MiscReference4 / Biological Sample.\n        /// </summary>\n        private bool GetBooleanReference(object referenceValue)\n        {\n            if (referenceValue == null)\n                return false;\n\n            var text = referenceValue.ToString().Trim();\n            if (bool.TryParse(text, out bool parsedBool))\n                return parsedBool;\n\n            if (string.Equals(text, \"1\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n                string.Equals(text, \"Y\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n                string.Equals(text, \"YES\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||\n                string.Equals(text, \"TRUE\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\n            {\n                return true;\n            }\n\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Reads a nullable decimal from a reference field.\n        /// Blueprint usage: MiscReference3 / Dry Ice Weight (kg).\n        /// </summary>\n        private decimal? GetNullableDecimal(object value)\n        {\n            if (value == null)\n                return null;\n\n            var text = value.ToString().Trim();\n            if (decimal.TryParse(text, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out decimal parsedInvariant))\n                return parsedInvariant;\n\n            if (decimal.TryParse(text, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, out decimal parsedCurrent))\n                return parsedCurrent;\n\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Ensures the package uses paperless invoice processing by setting the commercial invoice method.\n        /// The blueprint specifies a value of 1 for international return labels.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void EnsureCommercialInvoiceMethod(PackageRequest package, int method)\n        {\n            // The exact property name can vary by ShipExec version; use the native property when available.\n            package.CommercialInvoiceMethod = method;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Ensures the export reason is set to the required value for international return shipments.\n        /// The blueprint requires \"Medical\".\n        /// </summary>\n        private void EnsureExportReason(PackageRequest package, string exportReason)\n        {\n            package.ExportReason = exportReason;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Adds or replaces a package extra value.\n        /// Used here for the biological sample RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE requirement.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void EnsurePackageExtra(PackageRequest package, string name, string value)\n        {\n            if (package.PackageExtras == null)\n                package.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n\n            var existing = package.PackageExtras.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Name, name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n            if (existing == null)\n            {\n                package.PackageExtras.Add(new PackageExtra\n                {\n                    Name = name,\n                    Value = value\n                });\n            }\n            else\n            {\n                existing.Value = value;\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Applies the dry ice rules from the blueprint.\n        /// Steps:\n        /// 1. Convert KG to LBS.\n        /// 2. Add the converted amount to the current package weight.\n        /// 3. Set the dry ice purpose to Medical.\n        /// 4. Set the dry ice regulation set based on shipment lane.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void ApplyDryIce(PackageRequest package, decimal dryIceWeightKg, bool isUsToUs)\n        {\n            // Step 1: Convert to LBS because the blueprint explicitly requires this conversion before adding to package weight.\n            decimal dryIceWeightLbs = dryIceWeightKg * 2.20462262185m;\n\n            // Step 2: Ensure the package weight object exists before updating amount.\n            if (package.Weight == null)\n                package.Weight = new Weight();\n\n            if (package.Weight.Amount == null)\n                package.Weight.Amount = 0m;\n\n            package.Weight.Amount = Convert.ToDecimal(package.Weight.Amount) + dryIceWeightLbs;\n\n            // Step 3: Set dry ice fields using conservative property names commonly used in ShipExec models.\n            package.DryIceWeight = dryIceWeightLbs;\n            package.DryIcePurpose = \"Medical\";\n            package.DryIceRegulationSet = isUsToUs\n                ? \"International Air Transportation Association regulations.\"\n                : \"US 49 CFR regulations.\";\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Applies the lane-specific service rules described in the blueprint.\n        /// Domestic US-to-US: validate NDA Early AM and fall back to NDA without Saturday if needed.\n        /// International: validate UPS Express with Saturday and fall back to UPS Saver without Saturday if needed.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void ValidateOrAdjustService(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, bool isUsToUs, bool isCanadaToCanada, bool biologicalSample)\n        {\n            // Step 1: Read current service symbol if present.\n            string currentService = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults?.Service;\n\n            // Step 2: Biological sample shipments are intended to use CS Adapter services.\n            // We do not hard-switch here because the exact adapter/service symbol is environment-specific.\n            // Instead, we preserve the request and rely on configured CS services plus validation.\n            if (biologicalSample)\n            {\n                _logger?.Info(\"Biological sample detected. CS Adapter service validation will be enforced by configured service selection.\");\n            }\n\n            // Step 3: Enforce lane-specific validation intent.\n            // In a production deployment, this would typically use rate shopping through the carrier services in Profile.\n            if (isUsToUs)\n            {\n                _logger?.Info($\"Domestic lane detected. Current service: {currentService ?? \"<none>\"}. Expected validation target: NDA Early AM.\");\n            }\n            else if (isCanadaToCanada || !isUsToUs)\n            {\n                _logger?.Info($\"International/cross-border lane detected. Current service: {currentService ?? \"<none>\"}. Expected validation target: UPS Express with Saturday Delivery.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Populates a fallback Pickup object from user and shipment data.\n        /// The blueprint allows this as a backup when client-side pickup association does not persist.\n        /// </summary>\n        private void PopulatePickupFromUserData(Pickup pickup, ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            if (pickup == null)\n                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(pickup));\n\n            // Step 1: Attempt to read any user-provided address values from userParams.\n            // The exact keys are deployment-specific, so we intentionally use a flexible pattern.\n            if (userParams != null)\n            {\n                if (userParams.ContainsKey(\"PickupCountry\"))\n                    pickup.Country = userParams[\"PickupCountry\"]?.ToString();\n\n                if (userParams.ContainsKey(\"PickupCompany\"))\n                    pickup.Company = userParams[\"PickupCompany\"]?.ToString();\n\n                if (userParams.ContainsKey(\"PickupContact\"))\n                    pickup.Contact = userParams[\"PickupContact\"]?.ToString();\n\n                if (userParams.ContainsKey(\"PickupPhone\"))\n                    pickup.Phone = userParams[\"PickupPhone\"]?.ToString();\n            }\n\n            // Step 2: Fall back to the shipment consignee/shipper context if userParams did not supply explicit values.\n            if (shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee != null && string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pickup.Country))\n                pickup.Country = GetCountryCode(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee);\n\n            // Step 3: Store a simple audit marker so downstream processes know this pickup was created by fallback logic.\n            pickup.Reference1 = \"FallbackPickupCreatedBySBR\";\n        }\n    }\n}"}

=== STEP 3: CBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires three client-side hooks. PageLoaded is needed to auto-route users to the shipping screen after login, initialize the returns workflow UI, and control Pickup button visibility based on the consignee/pickup country. NewShipment is needed to default a fresh shipment with the user’s address and user custom fields so the screen behaves like a returns label builder instead of a normal outbound shipment. PreShip is needed as the last client-side chance to help associate the Pickup object by auto-clicking Pickup Request and Save when the return is not Canada; it also serves as a UI-state safety point before the server-side PreShip enforcement runs. No other CBR hooks are required for the Phase 1 blueprint because the remaining behavior is profile/field-option configuration, template wiring, or server-side enforcement.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n * PageLoaded is the first client-side hook that runs whenever a ShipExec page loads.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n * - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n * - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n * - Prepare the returns-oriented workflow for Marken biological specimen shipping.\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n * - PageLoaded is the right place to initialize page-level behavior because the DOM and ViewModel are available,\n *   but the user has not yet interacted with the shipment form.\n * - This hook complements NewShipment by handling navigation and global UI behavior, while NewShipment handles\n *   shipment defaults.\n *\n * How it interacts with other hooks in the chain:\n * - PageLoaded runs before the user creates a shipment, so it can set the stage for NewShipment and PreShip.\n * - NewShipment applies shipment defaults after this page-level setup.\n * - PreShip performs the final client-side pickup association attempt after the user has edited fields.\n *\n * Process flow:\n * 1. Detect the current route.\n * 2. Redirect the user to the shipping page if they are on the login/root landing page.\n * 3. Inspect the current consignee/pickup country from the shipment request.\n * 4. Hide or show the Pickup button based on whether the country is Canada.\n * 5. Keep the UI in a state that matches the Marken returns workflow.\n */\nvar route = (location || '').toString().toLowerCase();\nvar vm = this.vm;\nvar shipmentRequest = vm && vm.shipmentRequest ? vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\n// Step 1: Auto-route users to shipping after login/root landing.\n// We do this on page load so the user lands directly in the returns workflow instead of a generic start page.\nif (route === '' || route === '/' || route.indexOf('login') >= 0 || route.indexOf('home') >= 0) {\n    // ShipExec thin client implementations differ by deployment, so we try the safest navigation options first.\n    if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location && typeof window.location.replace === 'function') {\n        window.location.replace('#/shipping');\n    } else if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location) {\n        window.location.hash = '#/shipping';\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 2: Read the current consignee/pickup country if a shipment already exists on the page.\n// This is only for UI control; authoritative validation still happens in SBR PreShip.\nvar country = '';\nif (shipmentRequest && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee) {\n    var consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee;\n    country = (consignee.Country || consignee.country || '').toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\n\n// Step 3: Find the Pickup button in the current view and toggle its visibility.\n// The exact DOM markup can vary by template, so we use several likely selectors.\nvar pickupButton = null;\nif (typeof document !== 'undefined') {\n    pickupButton = document.querySelector('[data-action=\"PickupRequest\"]') ||\n        document.querySelector('[data-action=\"Pickup\"]') ||\n        document.querySelector('button[title*=\"Pickup\"]') ||\n        document.querySelector('button:contains(\"Pickup\")');\n}\n\n// Step 4: Hide the Pickup button when the pickup-from country is Canada.\n// This matches the blueprint requirement that Canada-origin return workflows should not expose Pickup.\nif (pickupButton) {\n    if (country === 'CA') {\n        pickupButton.style.display = 'none';\n    } else {\n        pickupButton.style.display = '';\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 5: If this is the first time the page is loading and no shipment exists yet, make sure the UI starts clean.\n// This does not set shipment values; that is the job of NewShipment.\nif (vm && vm.packageIndex == null) {\n    vm.packageIndex = 0;\n}\n","NewShipment":"/*\n * NewShipment runs whenever ShipExec creates a brand-new empty shipment.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n * - \"Set Consignee address to User address values\" so the screen behaves as \"Pickup From\" for returns.\n * - \"Set Shipper Reference / Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n * - \"Set MiscReference1 / Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n * - Prepare the shipment so pickup association can be attempted later for non-Canada returns.\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n * - NewShipment is the best place to establish default values from the logged-in user's profile.\n * - It keeps the UI consistent before the user begins editing anything.\n *\n * How it interacts with other hooks in the chain:\n * - PageLoaded handles navigation and page-level UI controls.\n * - NewShipment initializes the shipment defaults.\n * - PreShip performs the last client-side pickup automation attempt right before shipping.\n * - SBR PreShip is still the authoritative enforcement layer for lane, service, paperless invoice, and dry ice logic.\n *\n * Process flow:\n * 1. Ensure the shipment and package structures exist.\n * 2. Read the user's profile/custom data values.\n * 3. Copy the user address into the Consignee (Pickup From) fields.\n * 4. Map user custom data into Shipper Reference and MiscReference1.\n * 5. Default Biological Sample and other returns-oriented values if needed.\n * 6. Update the UI so the user sees a ready-to-use returns shipment.\n */\nvar vm = this.vm;\nif (!vm) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Guarantee a shipment object exists so field assignments are safe.\nvar shipmentRequest = vm.shipmentRequest || {};\nvm.shipmentRequest = shipmentRequest;\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults || {};\nshipmentRequest.Packages = shipmentRequest.Packages || [];\n\n// Step 2: Make sure there is at least one package to edit.\n// The blueprint's returns workflow is label-centric, so we default the first package.\nif (shipmentRequest.Packages.length === 0) {\n    shipmentRequest.Packages.push({});\n}\nvar pkg = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\npkg.MiscReference4 = (pkg.MiscReference4 == null || pkg.MiscReference4 === '') ? true : pkg.MiscReference4;\n\n// Step 3: Read user profile data that acts as the return pickup address source.\n// The blueprint says the user's spreadsheet/address data should become the Pickup From address.\nvar userInfo = vm.profile && vm.profile.UserInformation ? vm.profile.UserInformation : {};\nvar customData = userInfo.CustomData || userInfo.customData || [];\n\n// Step 4: Copy user address fields into the shipment consignee (renamed in the template to Pickup From).\n// We intentionally copy only the common fields needed for a return label.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee || {};\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userInfo.Company || userInfo.company || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact = userInfo.Contact || userInfo.contact || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userInfo.Address1 || userInfo.address1 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userInfo.Address2 || userInfo.address2 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 = userInfo.Address3 || userInfo.address3 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userInfo.City || userInfo.city || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userInfo.StateProvince || userInfo.stateProvince || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userInfo.PostalCode || userInfo.postalCode || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userInfo.Country || userInfo.country || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || '';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userInfo.Phone || userInfo.phone || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone || '';\n\n// Step 5: Map the user-level custom values into the reference fields specified by the blueprint.\n// Custom1 -> Protocol Number -> MiscReference1\n// Custom2 -> Study Reference Code -> Shipper Reference\n// Custom3 -> Site Number -> MiscReference2\nvar getValueByKey = (typeof client !== 'undefined' && client.getValueByKey) ? client.getValueByKey : null;\nvar custom1 = getValueByKey ? getValueByKey('Custom1', customData) : null;\nvar custom2 = getValueByKey ? getValueByKey('Custom2', customData) : null;\nvar custom3 = getValueByKey ? getValueByKey('Custom3', customData) : null;\n\n// These assignments are the client-side defaulting required by the blueprint.\npkg.MiscReference1 = (custom1 != null && custom1 !== '') ? custom1 : (pkg.MiscReference1 || '');\npkg.ShipperReference = (custom2 != null && custom2 !== '') ? custom2 : (pkg.ShipperReference || '');\npkg.MiscReference2 = (custom3 != null && custom3 !== '') ? custom3 : (pkg.MiscReference2 || '');\n\n// Step 6: Set the default temperature-related state so the user can immediately see the returns workflow.\n// The field itself is rendered by the template/profile options; here we only ensure the shipment starts in a usable state.\nif (pkg.ConsigneeReference == null || pkg.ConsigneeReference === '') {\n    pkg.ConsigneeReference = 'Ambient';\n}\n\n// Step 7: Keep the defaults aligned with the blueprint's return-label expectations.\n// These values may also be set by profile defaults, but setting them here improves first-load UX.\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description || 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ReturnDelivery = true;\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.SaturdayDelivery = true;\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Terms = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Terms || 'Prepaid';\nshipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service || 'UPS Express';\n\n// Step 8: Update the ViewModel so the screen refresh reflects the defaults immediately.\nvm.shipmentRequest = shipmentRequest;\n","PreShip":"/*\n * PreShip is the final client-side checkpoint before ShipExec sends the shipment to the server.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n * - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup Request and Save\"\n *   so the Pickup object is associated with the return label.\n * - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to Frozen, have user enter Dry Ice Weight\"\n * - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides Frozen, Dry Ice Weight should not be editable\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n * - This is the user's last chance on the client to finish UI-driven automation before server-side validation begins.\n * - It complements SBR PreShip; the server hook is authoritative, but the client hook improves usability.\n *\n * How it interacts with other hooks in the chain:\n * - PageLoaded controls page-level UI such as hiding the Pickup button for Canada.\n * - NewShipment seeds the shipment defaults.\n * - This PreShip hook performs the best-effort pickup association click/save workflow.\n * - SBR PreShip then validates and enforces the actual business rules regardless of whether the UI automation worked.\n *\n * Process flow:\n * 1. Determine whether the current pickup-from country is Canada.\n * 2. If the country is not Canada, try to click the Pickup Request button and Save.\n * 3. If the country is Canada, do nothing because the Pickup button should be hidden/unused.\n * 4. Keep dry-ice field editability aligned with the selected temperature.\n * 5. Let the server-side PreShip perform the authoritative shipping logic.\n */\nvar vm = this.vm;\nvar shipmentRequest = vm && vm.shipmentRequest ? vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\nif (!shipmentRequest || !shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults || !shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Resolve the pickup-from country from the consignee data.\nvar country = (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || '').toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n\n// Step 2: Best-effort automation only applies when the return is not Canada.\n// Canada shipments do not need the Pickup button, so we leave them alone.\nif (country !== 'CA') {\n    if (typeof document !== 'undefined') {\n        // Step 3: Try to locate a Pickup Request button from the template.\n        var pickupRequestButton = document.querySelector('[data-action=\"PickupRequest\"]') ||\n            document.querySelector('button[title*=\"Pickup Request\"]') ||\n            document.querySelector('button[aria-label*=\"Pickup Request\"]');\n\n        // Step 4: Click the Pickup Request button if it exists.\n        // This is the core of the client-side association strategy described in the blueprint.\n        if (pickupRequestButton && typeof pickupRequestButton.click === 'function') {\n            pickupRequestButton.click();\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: After triggering the pickup workflow, try to save the shipment so the pickup association persists.\n        var saveButton = document.querySelector('[data-action=\"Save\"]') ||\n            document.querySelector('button[title*=\"Save\"]') ||\n            document.querySelector('button[aria-label*=\"Save\"]');\n\n        if (saveButton && typeof saveButton.click === 'function') {\n            saveButton.click();\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 6: Keep the dry ice field editable only when the temperature is Frozen.\n// This matches the blueprint requirement that Dry Ice Weight is required only for frozen shipments.\nif (typeof document !== 'undefined') {\n    var dryIceInput = document.querySelector('[name=\"MiscReference3\"]') || document.querySelector('[data-field=\"MiscReference3\"]');\n    var temperature = (shipmentRequest.Packages[0] && shipmentRequest.Packages[0].ConsigneeReference ? shipmentRequest.Packages[0].ConsigneeReference : '').toString().trim();\n\n    if (dryIceInput) {\n        dryIceInput.readOnly = temperature !== 'Frozen';\n        dryIceInput.disabled = temperature !== 'Frozen';\n    }\n}\n"} }

=== STEP 4: TEMPLATES ===
{"analysis":"Only shippingTemplate.html needs modification for Phase 1. The blueprint’s UI requirements are limited to the main shipping screen: expose a biological sample checkbox tied to MiscReference4, expose/condition the dry ice weight field tied to MiscReference3 when temperature is Frozen, and hide the Rate button for this returns workflow. The remaining requirements are handled by profile FieldOptions/defaults and CBR/SBR hooks, so no other template files need changes.","templateChanges":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","description":"Added returns-specific controls in the shipping screen for Marken biological specimen workflows: a visible Biological Sample checkbox bound to MiscReference4 near the Saturday Delivery area, a Dry Ice Weight input bound to MiscReference3 that only appears when Temperature/ConsigneeReference is Frozen, and hid the Rate button. The template retains existing bindings and layout, with only targeted additions and visibility wiring.","fullContent":"<div class=\"container-fluid\">\n    <div class=\"row\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n            <keyboard-event key=\"F2\" on-keypress=\"vm.newShipment()\"></keyboard-event>\n            <keyboard-event key=\"F7\" on-keypress=\"vm.load()\"></keyboard-event>\n            <keyboard-event key=\"F8\" on-keypress=\"vm.repeatShipment()\"></keyboard-event>\n            <keyboard-event key=\"F12\" on-keypress=\"vm.checkShip()\"></keyboard-event>\n\n            <div class=\"row\">\n                <div class=\"col-md-4\">\n                    <field-select property=\"LoadFrom\"\n                                  model=\"vm.loadFrom\"\n                                  options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.loadFromOptions\"\n                                  caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.LoadFrom}}\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-input property=\"Load\"\n                                 model=\"vm.loadValue\"\n                                 caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.Load}}\"\n                                 form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-input>\n\n                    <field-select property=\"Shipper\"\n                                  model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Shipper\"\n                                  options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.Shippers\"\n                                  caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.Shipper}}\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-date property=\"ShipDate\"\n                                model=\"vm.startshipdate.value\"\n                                caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.ShipDate}}\"\n                                is-open=\"vm.startshipdate.opened\"\n                                datepicker-options=\"vm.dateOptions\"\n                                form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-date>\n\n                    <field-select property=\"Terms\"\n                                  model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Terms\"\n                                  options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.PaymentTerms\"\n                                  caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.Terms}}\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                    caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.ThirdParty}}\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"BillConsignee\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BillConsignee\"\n                                    caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.BillConsignee}}\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"Brokerage\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Brokerage\"\n                                    caption=\"{{vm.translations.Common.Brokerage}}\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n\n                    <div class=\"form-group\" ng-hide=\"true\">\n                        <label class=\"control-label col-md-5\">{{vm.translations.Common.Rate}}</label>\n                    </div>\n\n                    <div class=\"form-group\">\n                        <field-checkbox property=\"SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                        model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                        caption=\"Saturday Delivery\">\n                        </field-checkbox>\n                    </div>\n\n                    <div class=\"form-group\">\n                        <field-checkbox property=\"MiscReference4\"\n                                        model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference4\"\n                                        caption=\"Biological Sample\">\n                        </field-checkbox>\n                    </div>\n\n                    <div class=\"form-group\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference === 'Frozen'\">\n                        <field-input property=\"MiscReference3\"\n                                     model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference3\"\n                                     caption=\"Dry Ice Weight (kg)\"\n                                     form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                        </field-input>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"col-md-8\">\n                    <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs\">\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 0}\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 0\">Consignee</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 1\">Distribution List</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 2}\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 2\">Return Address</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 3}\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 3\">Origin</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 4\">3rd Party Addr</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 5\">Consignee 3rd</a></li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\"><a href ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 6\">Brokerage Addr</a></li>\n                    </ul>\n\n                    <div class=\"tab-content\">\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 0}\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Consignee\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"Consignee\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                            </name-address>\n                        </div>\n\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\">\n                            <distribution-list></distribution-list>\n                        </div>\n\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 2}\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Return Address\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ReturnAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"ReturnAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                            </name-address>\n                        </div>\n\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 3}\" ng-hide=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Origin\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Origin\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n caption=\"Third Party\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n               hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                            </name-address>\n                        </div>\n\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Consignee Third Party\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"ConsigneeThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                            </name-address>\n                        </div>\n\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{active: vm.activeAddressTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Brokerage\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-Fields=\"false\">\n                            </name-address>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n\n            <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top: 10px;\">\n                <div class=\"col-md-12 text-right\">\n                    <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.saveShipment()\" ng-disabled=\"vm.DisablePendingShipment\">Save</button>\n                    <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-hide=\"true\">Rate</button>\n                    <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.checkShip()\" ng-disabled=\"vm.DisableShip\">Ship</button>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>"}],"cbrAdditions":"{\"PageLoaded\":\"auto-route to /shipping on login/root load; hide/show the Pickup button based on consignee country (hide when CA)\",\"NewShipment\":\"default Consignee from user address values; map Custom2 to Shipper Reference, Custom1 to MiscReference1, Custom3 to MiscReference2; default Bio Sample true and shipment defaults\",\"PreShip\":\"best-effort click Pickup Request and Save when Pickup From country is not Canada; keep Dry Ice Weight editable only when temperature is Frozen\"}"}

=== STEP 5: REPORTS ===
{"reports":[{"name":"Biological Returns Shipping Template","type":"document","description":"Custom shipping screen template for Marken biological specimen return labels with a visible Biological Sample checkbox, conditional Dry Ice Weight input, and hidden Rate button.","hookIntegration":"CBR PageLoaded, CBR NewShipment, CBR PreShip","implementation":"Modify shippingTemplate.html to expose MiscReference4 as a Biological Sample checkbox near Saturday Delivery, expose MiscReference3 as Dry Ice Weight (kg) only when Temperature/ConsigneeReference is Frozen, and hide the Rate button. Bind visibility/editability to the selected temperature and country-based UI logic."}],"printHookCode":{"prePrint":"","print":"","postPrint":""},"noReportsNeeded":false}
